title: Backlog Engine V1 — mutate commands + migrate legacy archive

stories.title: `npm run backlog:next` — pick the first unblocked story
stories.summary: Walk the active sprint, return the first story whose dependsOn ids all resolve to `implemented` (or are absent). `--json` mode for agents. Exits 0 with a story id on stdout; exits 1 with no output when nothing is ready (sprint blocked or fully done).
verification: npm run backlog:next — returns BACKLOG-CLI-MUTATE first (BACKLOG-NEXT itself is implemented)
npm run backlog:next -- --json — returns parseable JSON
npm run backlog:next -- --json returns parseable JSON

stories.title: `backlog:claim` / `backlog:done` / `backlog:defer` — story write commands
stories.summary: Three small CLI commands that mutate the active sprint JSON instead of hand-edits. `claim <story>` → status:in_progress. `done <story> --verification "..."` → status:implemented + verification[]. `defer <story> --reason "..."` → status:deferred + deferredReason. All run `backlog:check` after the write; reject on validation failure. Archive promotion stays manual for now (one explicit step).
verification: round-trip: claim → done → backlog:check → backlog:render --check all pass
round-trip: backlog:done BACKLOG-NEXT → backlog:check pass → backlog:render writes → backlog:next picks BACKLOG-CLI-MUTATE

stories.title: `backlog:pr-body` — render PR summary from sprint JSON
stories.summary: Stdout: a Markdown PR body summarising implemented stories + follow-ups + verification, formatted the way the WebGPU-sprint PRs were written by hand. `gh pr create` can pipe directly to its `--body-file -` argument. Optional `--sprint S079` flag for closing a non-active sprint (useful when archived just before push).
verification: npm run backlog:pr-body | head -20 — produces a sensible Markdown summary for S079
npm run backlog:pr-body renders Markdown body for active sprint S079 with all sections

stories.title: engine doctor — backlog section
stories.summary: Extend `engine doctor` with a `Backlog:` section reporting: active sprint id + title, count of pending / in_progress / implemented stories, blockers (open stories with unfinished dependencies), stories with empty verification once implemented. Diagnostic codes `AGF_BACKLOG_*` matching `backlog:check`.
verification: npm run engine:doctor -- examples/hello-3d — contains a Backlog section
running with a deliberately broken sprint surfaces AGF_BACKLOG_BLOCKED
npm run engine:doctor -- examples/hello-3d shows Backlog section with active sprint id, story counts, AGF_BACKLOG_BLOCKED count
synthetic broken dep surfaces AGF_BACKLOG_BLOCKED: 1 blocked - <story> ← needs [<missing>]

stories.title: Parse legacy `BACKLOG_ARCHIVE.md` prelude into sprint JSON files
stories.summary: One-shot import: parse the hand-written S0–S77 prelude into `backlog/sprints/S000.sprint.json` … `S077.sprint.json` files (best-effort — completed-work bullets become stories with empty deliverables/verification, sprint-level Verification/Deliverables/Follow-Ups become sprint `notes[]` / `followUps[]`). Renderer keeps the prelude untouched so the diff stays reviewable; once the JSON output looks acceptable, a follow-up sprint can drop the prelude.
verification: backlog/sprints/S000.sprint.json … S077.sprint.json exist and pass backlog:check
npm run backlog:migrate-history writes 73 sprint files (S000-S072)
npm run backlog:check exits 0 (warnings only: AGF_BACKLOG_ARCHIVED_NO_DATE)
npm run backlog:render writes rendered archive tail covering 73 imported sprints

followUps: BACKLOG-MIGRATE-HISTORY-CLEANUP — once the imported JSON renders well, drop the hand-written prelude from `BACKLOG_ARCHIVE.md` and let the renderer own the entire file.
BACKLOG-PRECOMMIT — wire `backlog:check` + `backlog:render -- --check` into the preflight script.
outOfScope: GitHub Issues / Linear sync — still repo-local v1.
Atomic claim for parallel agents — single primary agent for now.
